content: A parallel plate capacitor with plate area $A$ and separation between plates $d$ is charged with constant current $I$. Consider a plane surface of area $\frac{5 A}{6}$ parallel to the plates and drawn between the plates. The displacement current through area is
explanation:
The charge on the capacitor plates at time $t$ is given by $q=I t$

Electric field $=\varepsilon=\frac{\sigma}{\varepsilon_0}=\frac{q}{A \varepsilon_0}=\frac{I t}{A \varepsilon_0}$

Flux through area $\frac{5 \mathrm{~A}}{6}$ is $\phi_{\varepsilon}=\left(\frac{5 \mathrm{~A}}{6}\right) \varepsilon$

$$ \Rightarrow \quad \phi_{\varepsilon}=\frac{5 \mathrm{~A}}{6} \times \frac{I t}{A \varepsilon_0} \Rightarrow \phi_{\varepsilon}=\frac{5 I t}{6 \varepsilon_0} $$

Now, displacement current $=I_d=\varepsilon_0 \frac{d \phi_e}{d t}$

                                         $$ \begin{aligned} & I_d=\varepsilon_0 \frac{d}{d t}\left[\frac{5 I t}{6 \varepsilon_0}\right] \\ & I_d=\frac{5}{6} I \end{aligned} $$

content: The electric and the magnetic field associated with an E.M. wave, propagating along the $$\mathrm{Z}$$-axis can be represented by
explanation: The direction of propagation of EM waves is always perpendicular to both the electric and magnetic fields. Therefore, $$+v e~ x$$ and $$+v e~ y$$ directions should be the directions of the electric and magnetic fields, respectively.

content: An electromagnetic wave is propagating along X-axis. At x = 1 cm and t = 18s, its electric vector |E| = 8 V/m, then the magnitude of its magnetic vector is
explanation:
As we know that, $${E \over B} = c$$

so, $$B = {E \over c} = {8 \over {3 \times {{10}^8}}} = 2.66 \times {10^{ - 8}}$$

options.content: The speed of light in the meta-material is $$v = c|n|$$.
The speed of light in the meta-material is $$v = {c \over {|n|}}$$.
The speed of light in the meta-material is v = c.
The wavelength of the light in the meta-material ($$\lambda$$<sub>m</sub>) is given by $$\lambda$$<sub>m</sub> = $$\lambda$$<sub>air</sub> | n |.

explanation:
When light travel in meta-material, physical characteristics remain unchanged.

$$\therefore$$ $$v = {c \over {|n|}}$$

where, | n | is the relative refractive index of meta-material.

As, $$n = {c \over v} \Rightarrow |n| = {{{n_{med}}} \over {{n_{air}}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left| n \right| = {{{\lambda _{air}}} \over {{\lambda _{med}}}} \Rightarrow {\lambda _{med}} = {{{\lambda _{air}}} \over {\left| n \right|}}$$

content:
The magnetic field of a beam emerging from a fitter facing a flood light is given by

B = 10 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$8 sin(1 $$\times$$ 107z $$-$$ 3.6 $$\times$$ 1015t)T. The average intensity of the beam is
explanation:
Given, magnetic field,

B = 10 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$8 sim(1 $$\times$$ 107z $$-$$ 3.6 $$\times$$ 1015t) T ......... (i)

Standard equation of magnetic field,

B = B0 sin(kz $$-$$ $$\omega$$t) ....... (ii)

On comparing Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

B0 = 10 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$8 T

The average intensity of the beam,

$${I_{av}} = {{B_0^2} \over {2{\mu _0}}} = {1 \over 2} \times {{{{(10 \times {{10}^{ - 8}})}^2} \times 3 \times {{10}^8}} \over {4\pi \times {{10}^{ - 7}}}}$$ [$$\because$$ $$c = 3 \times {10^8}$$ ms$$-$$1]

= 1.19 W/m2
